description_long: This object is a rectangular lotion bottle with a tall, narrow neck and a rounded shoulder leading up to a cylindrical cap. The body of the bottle is flat on the front and back, with slightly curved sides, giving it a classic apothecary or medicinal bottle appearance. The front face is smooth and unadorned, while the back face features embossed text, adding a tactile and visual detail. The cap appears to be a simple cylindrical closure, likely screw-on, matching the bottle's material and color. The entire object is a uniform, matte white, with no visible graphics or color variations. The bottle is likely made of glass or plastic, designed to hold liquid or cream lotion, and is compact enough to be handheld.
description: A matte white, rectangular lotion bottle with a tall neck, cylindrical cap, and embossed text on the back.
description_view_0: Front view shows a flat, smooth face with a rounded shoulder and cylindrical cap, no visible text or graphics.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the bottle's narrow profile and the cylindrical shape of the neck and cap.
description_view_2: Back view displays embossed text running vertically along the bottle's surface, otherwise similar to the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the narrow side and the continuous cylindrical neck.
